title: Understanding the Motivational Factors on Online Payment Practices
author: Pathak, S.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Understanding the factors infuencing online payment practices among undergraduate students in Nepal is imperative due to the country's dependence on digital transactions and the changing fnancial technology landscape. This study aims to clarify the impact of security measures, user experience, transaction fees and costs, and network connectivity on online payment practices among undergraduate students in Bharatpur Metropolitan City, Chitwan. Our study seeks to comprehensively understand the opinions and experiences of undergraduate students regarding online payment practices using a quantitative approach. The sample population consisted of Two Hundred and Sixty (N = 260) undergraduate students enrolled in Bharatpur Metropolitan City. A random sampling method was used to select the sample population for this study. This study's ethical issues were carefully considered during the research process. We expect our results to establish the associations between security measures, user experience, transaction fees and costs, and network connectivity and their impacts on online payment practices. The implications of this study are far-reaching and extend to educational institutions, researchers, organisational leaders, educators, new researchers, and students alike. Insights gleaned from the study can inform policy decisions, academic interventions, and industry practices aimed at enhancing online payment practices and promoting fnancial literacy among undergraduate students in Nepal. Keywords: bharatpur metropolitan city, factors infuencing, implications, online payment practices, quantitative approach, undergraduate students
